Even a delay in holding the identification parade of over a year was not fatal to the idcntificr corrcctlv identifying the accused ln this respect reliance is placed on the case of Solat Ali Khan v. The State (2002 SCMI{ 820) and in this case the irlentificatiorl parade was carried out after 20 months ancl in an;', 10 Q4T +6t event evidence flowing from an identification p;rracle is only a corroborative piece of evidence and cannot supersede reliable, trustworthy, confidence inspiring direct evidence. In this respect relianct' is placed on Muhammad Ehsan v. The State (2006 scMR 1857).
